question: Did the model generate exactly two images as explicitly requested in the instructions?
Does the generated text specifically explain the aerodynamics of the vehicle's teardrop shape?
Does the generated text explicitly explain the interaction logic (e.g., gesture, voice, or eye control) used to operate the vehicle?
Does the text use appropriate industrial design and automotive terminology (e.g., drag coefficient, haptic feedback, ergonomics)?
Does the first generated image clearly depict a vehicle with a teardrop-shaped body?
Is the first generated image set against a minimalist white studio background?
Does the first image render the vehicle with a 'silver liquid metal' texture (highly reflective, fluid-like surface)?
Does the first image depict a magnetic levitation hubless wheel design (wheels without central hubs, spokes, or axles)?
Does the second generated image depict an interior cabin view of the vehicle?
Does the second image explicitly exclude a traditional steering wheel (no circular wheel, yoke, or steering column)?
Does the second image clearly display a holographic projection interactive interface?
Does the second image feature an ergonomic zero-gravity seat design (reclined or body-contouring)?
Are both generated images free of obvious AI artifacts such as warped geometry, incoherent structural elements, or distorted reflections?
Does the visual content of the exterior image match the specific details described in the adjacent text (e.g., material finish, shape)?
Does the visual content of the interior image match the interaction logic described in the adjacent text (e.g., interface type)?
Do the two generated images maintain a consistent visual style and futuristic aesthetic appropriate for a single concept car pitch?
Are the images placed immediately adjacent to their corresponding text sections (exterior image near exterior description, interior image near interior description)?
